texto: Life is a funny thing. We claim it to be our own; but the truth is, it's not. It belongs to something much bigger. We, like everything else, are transient. This life is temporary and everything about us is temporary. What we call our life is nothing more than borrowed energy from something much bigger--nature, the universe, God--whatever floats your boat. And one day, when we pass, we will give that energy back to the world we borrowed it from in the first place.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Leanne Waters: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Life is a funny thing. We claim it to be our own; but the truth is, it's not. It belongs to somet…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, energy, vida pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de vida e recovery.
